key: OpenAI GPT-4.1 context window official OpenAI API documentation|5

value.title: Large context window — what are you using it for? - Community
value.content: Was just reminded that GPT-4.1 has the largest context window of all the OpenAI models — up to 1,047,576 tokens. I’ve not done anything yet… but I’m excited to send some of my longer novels (100k+ words) and see if I can get some good info on them. But will definitely try to see if it can be used in doc generations based on large API definitions and database schemas. Hey Edwin, just was playing with this documentation and from what I saw, unfortunately the large context window even if used only to provide context info, does not bring better results when used in specific tasks (several files concatenated, containing tech info) : task was to generate a documentation for an entity in the database- fields, purpose, translations, along with its relations and business logic. Even if all the info was available in the context and the expected output was under 5k tokens, the GPT4.1 fails to consider the required context and zaps a lot of otherwise simple elements from it.

value.title: GPT-4.1 | OpenAI 1M Context LLM API Pricing | WaveSpeedAI
value.content: GPT-4.1 is a flagship large language model optimized for advanced instruction following, real-world software engineering, and long-context reasoning.

value.title: GPT-4.1 API Tutorial: Explore OpenAI's NEW Models (Mini, Nano, 1M Tokens!)
value.content:
GPT-4.1 API Tutorial: Explore OpenAI's NEW Models (Mini, Nano, 1M Tokens!)
Shane | LLM Implementation
2370 subscribers
6 likes
312 views
15 Apr 2025
🚀 Unlock the power of OpenAI's latest GPT-4.1 models! Learn how the new API featuring GPT-4.1, GPT-4.1 mini & GPT-4.1 nano (with 1 Million token context!) revolutionizes coding, context handling, and cost efficiency. Get started with the API and Playground today!

In this video, we break down the exciting updates to the OpenAI model lineup.

⏱️ TIMESTAMPS / CHAPTERS:
0:00 Introduction to GPT-4.1
0:17 What's New: Overview of the GPT-4.1 family (Mini, Nano included!)
0:24 Key Features: Coding, Instruction Following, 1M Context, Knowledge Cutoff
0:41 Performance Boost: Coding (SWE-bench +21.4%)
2:54 Performance Boost: Instruction Following (MultiChallenge +10.5%)
3:36 Performance Boost: Long Context & Video (Video-MME +6.7%)
5:45 Model Deep Dive: GPT-4.1 Mini (vs GPT-4o)
6:24 Model Deep Dive: GPT-4.1 Nano (Fast & Cheap)
6:32 Pricing Update: Cost Comparison & Prompt Caching Discount
7:29 How to Use: OpenAI Playground Demo
8:18 How to Use: Python API Tutorial (Google Colab)

This video covers:
*   Major improvements in coding benchmarks (SWE-bench).
*   Enhanced instruction following capabilities (MultiChallenge).
*   Massive 1 Million token context windows.
*   A June 2024 knowledge cutoff.
*   Deep dives into GPT-4.1 Mini and Nano, comparing speed, cost, and intelligence.
*   Updated pricing details (26% cheaper median than GPT-4o) and the new prompt caching discount.
*   How to access and use the models via the Playground and the Python API.

This video is perfect for developers using the OpenAI API, AI researchers, and anyone interested in the cutting edge of large language models (LLMs). See how these new models push the boundaries!

🔗 Relevant Links:
OpenAI Announcement: https://openai.com/index/gpt-4-1/
OpenAI API Documentation: https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/chat/create?lang=python
OpenAI Playground (GPT-4.1 Mini example): https://platform.openai.com/docs/models/gpt-4.1-mini
SWE-bench: https://www.swebench.com/#verified
MultiChallenge Benchmark: https://scale.com/leaderboard/multichallenge
Video-MME Benchmark: https://video-mme.github.io/home_page.html

👍 If you found this video helpful, please like and subscribe for more AI updates and tutorials! Let us know in the comments what you think of GPT-4.1!

#OpenAI #GPT41 #GPT41mini #GPT41nano #API #LLM #ArtificialIntelligence #Coding #LongContext #AIModel #Developer #Tutorial #AIBenchmarks #Python #GoogleColab #OpenAIPlayground

value.title: Introducing GPT-4.1 in the API - OpenAI
value.content: *   **Coding**: GPT‑4.1 scores 54.6% on [SWE-bench Verified](https://openai.com/index/introducing-swe-bench-verified/), improving by 21.4%abs over GPT‑4o and 26.6%abs over GPT‑4.5—making it a leading model for coding. *   **Instruction following:**On [Scale’s MultiChallenge⁠(opens in a new window)](https://scale.com/leaderboard/multichallenge) benchmark, a measure of instruction following ability, GPT‑4.1 scores 38.3%, a 10.5%abs increase over GPT‑4o. When combined with primitives like the [Responses API⁠(opens in a new window)](https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/responses), developers can now build agents that are more useful and reliable at real-world software engineering, extracting insights from large documents, resolving customer requests with minimal hand-holding, and other complex tasks. In ChatGPT, many of the improvements in instruction following, coding, and intelligence have been gradually incorporated into the [latest version⁠(opens in a new window)](https://help.openai.com/en/articles/6825453-chatgpt-release-notes) of GPT‑4o, and we will continue to incorporate more with future releases. GPT‑4.5 was [introduced](https://openai.com/index/introducing-gpt-4-5/) as a research preview to explore and experiment with a large, compute-intensive model, and we’ve learned a lot from developer feedback. To aid in reproducing and contextualizing our results, we describe our setup for GPT‑4.1_[_here_⁠(opens in a new window)](https://cookbook.openai.com/examples/gpt4-1_prompting_guide)_.
